session.title: Sessions
session.emptyHint: Create one for the accordion on the bench, or just open the tuner: it works without a session.

card.changed: The accordion on the shelf has changed since this session was recorded.

dialog.name: Session name
dialog.nameHint: Whatever you will recognise it by: the accordion, or whose it is
dialog.a4Hint: This accordion's own A. Measure it before you assume 440.
dialog.instrumentHint: Pick the accordion and its reed ranks are set for you. Leave it if you do not know: the tuner works either way.
dialog.says: Reed ranks {banks}, and {registers} register. Set for you: you never have to say which rank a reading came from. | Reed ranks {banks}, and {registers} registers. Set for you: you never have to say which rank a reading came from.
dialog.saysNothing: No reed ranks named, so the tuning table numbers its columns instead of naming them. That is fine, and you can pick an accordion later.

error.saveFailed: Could not save the session. Your last takes may be lost if you close now.
error.invalidA4: The reference pitch must be between 430 and 450 Hz.
error.invalidReedCount: An accordion sounds between 1 and 8 reeds on a note.
error.invalidNote: That note is outside the range the tuner tracks.
error.invalidReed: This accordion does not sound that reed.
error.invalidUnit: Values are entered in cents or hertz.
error.invalidValue: That value is not a number.
error.fitFailed: Could not fit a curve to that pass.
error.noCurve: This session has no goal curve.
error.invalidInstrument: That accordion cannot be used: a reed rank is named twice, or a register sounds a rank the accordion does not have.
error.noRegister: This accordion has no such register.
error.instrumentName: Give the accordion a name so you can find it again.
error.notAnImage: That file is not a photograph.
error.hasReadings: This session already holds readings: they were all measured against its reference and reed count, so neither can change now.
error.noReadings: Nothing has been recorded in this session yet.
error.noBassMachine: This accordion declares no bass machine.
error.noBassRegister: The accordion has no such bass register.

session.deleteHint: Everything recorded through this accordion goes with it: every pass, every reading, and the goal curve. It cannot be recovered from audio, and it cannot be undone.

readings.empty: Nothing recorded yet. Open the tuner and play the accordion: what you catch lands here.

instrument.title: Accordions
instrument.emptyHint: An accordion is described once and used for every session on it: its reed ranks and its switches. Import one somebody sent you, or describe the one on your bench.
instrument.name: Name
instrument.nameHint: What you call this accordion. Yours to name; nothing translates it.
instrument.nameRequired: Give it a name so you can find it again
instrument.registersHint: Click the dots of each switch's symbol - the circle engraved on the accordion - or pick a common one. What it sounds is read straight off the dots.
instrument.registersSubtitle: The treble-side switches, and the ranks each one sounds.
instrument.says: Reed ranks {banks}. The widest switch sounds {reeds} reed. | Reed ranks {banks}. The widest switch sounds {reeds} reeds.
instrument.deleteHint: The description and its photograph go. Sessions already recorded on it are untouched: each one carries its own copy of the accordion and will still open, still print, and still mean what it meant.

builder.none: No registers yet. Pick a common one, or build it on the dots.

range.label: Keyboard range
range.hint: Optional. The lowest and highest key. You can also set it by ear during calibration.
range.backwards: The keyboard runs low to high.

instrument.a4Hint: This accordion's tuning: 442 on an old box, 440 on a newer one. Every session on it starts from here.
instrument.toleranceHint: How many cents off a reed may be before it is called out of tune. This accordion's own; a concert box is held tighter than a folk one.

bass.hint: Declare how many voices the bass sounds; most machines have four or five.
bass.fixed: No bass registers: a fixed machine, every voice always sounds.

calibrate.title: Calibrate
calibrate.needsSession: Open a session first. Calibration measures onto the accordion a session is recorded on.

range.backwards: The highest came out below the lowest. Play them the other way round.

sweep.soloTeaches: Sweeping a single-voice register also teaches the app that voice's sound.
sweep.noRegisters: This accordion has no registers described. The sweep still runs; its readings land in numbered columns.
sweep.done: Swept. {n} reading on the card. | Swept. {n} readings on the card.
